incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 latency increased from 0.15s to 18s) due to database connection pool exhaustion caused by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout, lasting approximately 2 hours and 25 minutes at SEV-2 severity
root_cause_summary: HTTP client timeout configuration change from 10s to 30s caused database connection pool exhaustion by holding connections 3x longer, preventing connection recycling
root_cause_trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z increasing HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s
customer_impact_description: Customers experienced severe API performance degradation with response times increasing from normal sub-second levels to 15-18 seconds, along with intermittent timeout errors affecting API requests
resolution_action: Emergency rollback of configuration change, reverting HTTP client timeout from 30s back to 10s
resolution_confirmed_by: Metrics showed connection pool utilization dropped from 100% to 60% baseline, p99 latency recovered to 0.18s by 15:00 UTC, 500 errors ceased, CloudWatch logs showed 'Database connection pool recovered' at 15:05 UTC
data_gaps: Scope limited to api-gateway service only - no data on whether other services or regions were affected; No data on percentage of requests affected or total customer count impacted; Timeline discrepancy: metrics show recovery at 15:00 UTC but rollback deployed at 15:45 UTC - unclear why recovery preceded rollback deployment; No data on what specific customer-facing products depend on api-gateway; Connection pool utilization metric shows stable 0.61 baseline in summary but raw values show spike to 1.0 (100%) - inconsistency in metric reporting
internal_details_to_exclude: alice.engineer@example.com; john.dev@example.com; jane.dev@example.com; bob.sre@example.com; rds-prod-main; PR #12345; PR #12340; PR #12347; commit SHA abc123; commit SHA def456; commit SHA xyz789; #incidents Slack channel; api-gateway/config/http_client.yaml file path; notification-service deployment details; CloudWatch specific log context fields; Prometheus metric label details

incident_summary: Portal authentication service experienced a full outage for approximately 50 minutes due to session-service-prod becoming unreachable, preventing customer logins while core threat detection remained operational
root_cause_summary: session-service-prod became unreachable, blocking SSO token validation required for portal authentication
customer_impact_description: Customers were unable to log into the security portal for approximately 50 minutes. All login attempts failed during this period. Email threat detection and processing continued operating normally throughout the incident.
affected_functionality: Portal login and authentication; Access to portal dashboard and UI
unaffected_functionality: Email threat detection and processing (IES pipeline confirmed operational)
resolution_confirmed_by: CloudWatch log message 'session-service-prod connection restored, auth requests resuming' at 10:00 UTC; Prometheus metrics showing portal_auth_response_latency_seconds returned to baseline 0.22s at 10:00 UTC; engineer confirmation 'portal fully accessible again' at 10:05 UTC
data_gaps: Root cause of why session-service-prod became unreachable is not explained in available data - only that it failed and required restart; Geographic scope unknown - logs reference 'prod-us-east-1' but no confirmation whether other regions were affected or if service is single-region; Number of affected customers or failed login attempts not quantified; Whether session-service-prod experienced a crash, network partition, resource exhaustion, or other specific failure mode is not documented
internal_details_to_exclude: bob.sre@example.com; carol.dev@example.com; session-service-prod; session-service-prod.internal:8080; prod-us-east-1; portal-auth-service (internal service name); ies-pipeline (internal service name); commit abc1234; Slack channel thread; /aws/portal-auth-service log group

incident_summary: Remediation service experienced complete failure of automated remediation actions for approximately 2.5 hours due to Microsoft Graph API outage, with SEV-2 severity
root_cause_summary: Microsoft Graph API outage caused all remediation actions requiring Microsoft Graph integration to fail with 503 errors
root_cause_trigger: Microsoft Graph API service degradation (graph.microsoft.com returning HTTP 503)
customer_impact_description: Customers experienced complete failure of automated remediation actions for detected threats. While threat detection continued normally, any automated responses requiring Microsoft Graph integration (such as user account actions, mailbox operations, or Microsoft 365 security controls) could not execute and were queued for later processing
affected_functionality: Automated remediation actions requiring Microsoft Graph API; Real-time threat response automation
unaffected_functionality: Threat detection and analysis (IES email security pipeline); Security event monitoring; Manual remediation capabilities
resolution_action: No internal action required — incident resolved when Microsoft restored Graph API service availability
resolution_confirmed_by: CloudWatch logs showing HTTP 200 responses from graph.microsoft.com at 18:55 UTC; remediation action latency returned to 1.7s baseline by 19:00 UTC; backlog processing confirmed in logs at 18:58 UTC
data_gaps: Exact number of failed/queued remediation actions during the outage; Specific types of remediation actions affected (user actions, mailbox operations, etc.); Number of customers impacted; Whether any manual workarounds were communicated to customers during the outage; Time to fully clear the backlog after recovery (logs show draining at 18:58 but completion time unknown)
internal_details_to_exclude: alice.engineer@example.com; bob.sre; prod-us-east-1; def5678; /aws/remediation-service; Slack usernames @alice.engineer and @bob.sre; graph.microsoft.com (internal API endpoint reference); us-east-1 region identifier
